# Resource: pipes_organization_service_account

Manage an organization-level service account. Service accounts are non-human users that can be used to authenticate automation within an organization.

## Example Usage

**Create an organization service account with a title and description**

```hcl
resource "pipes_organization_service_account" "example" {
  organization_handle = "my_org"
  title               = "Org CI/CD"
  description         = "Service account used by org-level CI pipelines"
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:

- `organization_handle` - (Required) The organization in which to create the service account. Changing this value forces a new resource; Terraform will delete the existing service account in the previous organization and create a new one in the target organization.
- `title` - (Optional) A friendly title for the service account.
- `description` - (Optional) A description for the service account.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to all arguments above, the following attributes are exported:

- `service_account_id` - The unique identifier of the service account.
- `tenant_id` - The tenant identifier that owns this service account.
- `handle` - The handle of the service account.
- `created_at` - Creation timestamp.
- `updated_at` - Last update timestamp.
- `version_id` - The current version of the service account.

## Import

Organization service accounts can be imported using an ID of the form `organization_handle/service_account_identifier`, e.g.,

```sh
terraform import pipes_organization_service_account.example my_org/u_01hkv1c8y8s6r2wqv2abcxyz
```
